subject: [RFC PATCH 28/40] mm: page_alloc: drive slab shrink from SPB anti-fragmentation pressure

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch introduces a new slab shrink mechanism to address anti-fragmentation pressure in the Linux kernel. The mechanism is triggered by various events, including demand and supply pressures, as well as proactive high-water marks. It uses a per-pgdat irq_work to bridge from allocator-lock context to a workqueue, which calls shrink_slab() with the zone's nid to free objects until either no progress is made or a batch of pageblock-sized objects has been freed. This approach aims to prevent sustained slab growth and fragmentation by releasing pressure on tainted SPBs, allowing for more efficient memory management.
